raw_response: This presentation suggests hemophilia, which typically presents as spontaneous or minor injury-induced joint bleeds along with gum bleeding. Hemophilia B (factor IX deficiency) has a higher prevalence among males and can be inherited from either parent through an affected male relative, fitting this family history pattern. Factor VIII deficiency would more commonly present without the specific family history noted here. Therefore, option B seems appropriate given the inheritance pattern described for both uncles. The correct answer is **B: IX**.
